content: Hi, I'm Devon. I've been dealing with this nagging back pain for months now and it's really affecting my work at the think tank.
Hello Devon, I'm sorry to hear about your back pain. As an AI ethics researcher, I imagine sitting at a desk for long periods might be challenging right now. Have you seen a specialist about it?
Yeah, I've been to two different chiropractors in the past 3 months. The first one in Cambridge where I live didn't help much, so I tried one closer to work in Boston last month.
That sounds frustrating. Have you considered physical therapy or maybe an ergonomic assessment of your workspace? Sometimes small adjustments can make a big difference.
I'm actually meeting with a physical therapist tomorrow morning at 9am. My wife Jenna suggested it - she's been amazing through all this. We have twin boys who are 8, so she's juggling a lot.
Jenna sounds wonderful. Twins at 8 years old must keep you both busy! How are you managing with your marathon training and hiking hobbies with the back pain?

Honestly, I had to pause the marathon training. Signed up for the Chicago marathon back in January, but I don't think I'll be able to do it this year. The hiking we've been doing is more like short walks in the Blue Hills Reservation now.
That's disappointing about the marathon. The Blue Hills Reservation is beautiful though - have you found any new sketching spots there since your mobility has changed?

Actually yes! There's a pond near the visitor center that's perfect for urban sketching. I've been going there on weekends while the boys are at soccer practice. Jenna drops them off and I get a couple hours to myself.
That sounds like a nice routine you've found. Having that creative outlet while managing your health challenges is important. Have you tried any specific pain management techniques that help?
